name: GLIOMA OF SPINAL CORD
definition: special variant of ependymoma of lower spinal cord variant of CNS ependymoma GANGLIOGLIONEUROMA = GANGLIONIC NEUROMA = NEUROASTROCYTOMA =

demographics.Frequency: 30% of spinal cord tumors; 2nd in prevalence to ependymoma in adults
demographics.Path: ill-defined fusiform cord enlargement without cleavage plane / capsule
demographics.Histo: hypercellularity with infiltrative growth along scaffold of normal astrocytes,
demographics.Location: thoracic cord (67%), cervical cord (49%), conus medullaris (3%); on average
demographics.Prevalence: 13% of all spinal ependymomas; most common neoplasm of conus

distribution: thoracic cord (67%), cervical cord (49%), conus medullaris (3%); on average

water-soluble myelographic contrast enters cystic space on delayed CT images

general: eccentric irregular tumor cysts + polar cysts + syrinx (common):
water-soluble myelographic contrast enters cystic space on delayed CT images
MR: usually extensive ill-defined homogeneous cord tumor with expansion of spinal cord:
iso- to hypointense to cord on T1WI
dilated veins on surface of cord
associated with at least one cyst (in 78-84%):
polar cyst (62%) at cranial and caudal aspect of tumor, which do not contain malignant
tumoral cyst (4-50%), which may contain tumor
enlarged cord with complete / partial block to flow of contrast material
iso- / hypointense (rarely hyperintense from hemorrhage) mass relative to spinal cord on
"cap" sign = extremely hypointense rim at the tumor poles on T2WI (in 20-33%) due to
mostly intense homogeneous enhancement (84%) with well-defined margins (89%)
isointense on T1WI + hyperintense on T2WI
occasionally hyperintense on T1WI + T2WI ← mucin content / hemorrhage
occasionally large lytic area of bone destruction
nonenhancing lesion with diffuse symmetric cord enlargement
scoliosis (44%), spinal remodeling (93%) ← relatively slow growth (rare in astrocytoma /
mixed tumor signal intensities on T1WI (in 84%)
surrounding edema (less common than in ependymoma / astrocytoma)
